description: Delivery of real-time alert payloads to registered mobile devices via a push proxy service, with per-device session targeting, JWT signing for security, and content-level controls from minimal...

actors.name: End User
actors.description: Receives push notifications on registered mobile devices

actors.name: Push Proxy Service
actors.description: Relay service that forwards signed notification payloads to APNS and FCM

actors.name: Notification System
actors.description: Evaluates eligibility, assembles payload, signs, and dispatches notifications

fields.name: device_id
fields.label: Unique identifier for the registered mobile device

fields.name: ack_id
fields.label: Per-delivery unique identifier for acknowledgement tracking

fields.name: message
fields.label: Notification body text; may be omitted in ID-loaded or generic modes

fields.name: badge
fields.label: Unread message count to display on the app badge

fields.name: channel_id
fields.label: Channel where the triggering message was posted

fields.name: sender_name
fields.label: Display name of the message author

fields.name: content_level
fields.label: How much message content to include in the payload

fields.name: signature
fields.label: ES256 JWT signature covering the ack_id and device_id to prevent spoofing

rules.rule_01: Push notifications are delivered independently to each active mobile session for the recipient user; each session receives its own uniquely signed payload with a distinct ack_id.
rules.rule_02: Notifications are suppressed entirely when the user's status is DND (Do Not Disturb); this check takes priority over all other preferences.
rules.rule_03: Push is also suppressed when the user is currently active in the same channel where the triggering message was posted (unless the notify-active-channel setting overrides this).
rules.rule_04: The id_loaded content level sends only the channel ID and post ID; the device fetches the full message content after receiving the notification, protecting content from being stored in push infrastructure.
rules.rule_05: Direct messages and group messages use "all" push logic regardless of the user's global push preference of "mention", because every DM is implicitly a personal communication.
rules.rule_06: Muted channels (mark_unread = mention) suppress push notifications for non-mention messages.
rules.rule_07: Each notification payload is signed with the server's asymmetric key using ES256; the push proxy validates the signature before forwarding to APNS or FCM.
rules.rule_08: When the push proxy responds with "remove", the device's push token is considered stale and the session is flagged for cleanup.
rules.rule_09: Plugin hooks can intercept, modify, or cancel push notifications before delivery.
rules.rule_10: Bots do not receive push notifications.

given: message triggers a notification for the recipient
recipient has at least one active mobile session with a registered device
user's push preference permits the notification type (all or mention when mentioned)
user is not in DND status
user is not active in the triggering channel (or notify-when-active is set)
no plugin hook rejected the notification
push_delivered.result: Signed payload delivered to push proxy; proxy forwards to APNS or FCM

push_suppressed_dnd.result: Notification not delivered; reason recorded as user_status

given: user is actively viewing the channel where the message was posted
push_suppressed_active_channel.result: Notification not delivered; user is already aware of the message

given: channel is muted for the recipient (mark_unread = mention)
message does not directly mention the user
push_suppressed_muted_channel.result: Notification not delivered; channel is muted

given: push proxy returns 'remove' response for a device

then.description: Session with the stale device token flagged for cleanup

push_device_stale.result: Stale device registration removed; future notifications skip this device

given: push proxy returns an error response
push_proxy_error.result: Delivery failure logged; no retry (stateless delivery model)

errors.message: Push notification could not be delivered. Please check your connection.
Mobile push notifications are not configured on this server.
Mobile push notifications require an active license for the hosted proxy service.

events.name: push.notification_sent
events.description: Push notification payload dispatched to the proxy service

events.name: push.notification_suppressed
events.description: Push notification eligible but suppressed for a documented reason

events.name: push.delivery_failed
events.description: Push proxy reported a delivery error

events.name: push.device_removed
events.description: Device registration removed because push proxy reported stale token

related.reason: User preferences and DND status are checked before every push delivery
Mobile sessions carry device IDs; session revocation removes device registrations
Email and push share notification trigger logic; the two complement each other

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Failed operations divided by total attempts

constraints.description: Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

tradeoffs.reason: notifications must reach recipients even if delayed

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
